name: coap-console

metadata.name: coap-console
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: CoAP client for interactive with remote serial input and output

documentation:
# coap-console: interactive access to serial input/output of CoAP servers

This Python package provides access
to resources that implement the `tag:riot-os.org,2021:ser-out` / `tag:riot-os.org,2021:ser-in` interfaces over CoAP.
Those resources can be used to provide log output
or interactive shell access
to CoAP servers (not only) on embedded systems.

It is mainly used together with the [coap-scroll-ring-server Rust crate]
which implements the server side,
for example as used in the [stdio example] on [RIOT OS]
and the [CoAP test of Ariel OS]
(which also serves as an example of how to use this securely).

It can be run directly from the source repository by running:

```
$ ./coap-console 'coap://[2001:db8::1]'
```

(which discovers a stdout resource or stdin-/out pair of resources on the given IP address)

or ran directly from git:

```
$ pipx run --spec "git+https://gitlab.com/chrysn/scroll-ring#egg=coap_console&subdirectory=coap-scroll-ring-server/tools/coap_console" coap-console 'coap://[2001:db8::1]'
```

or installed and run using:

```
$ pip install coap_console
$ coap-console 'coap://[2001:db8::1]'
```

(or ran directly from pip, or installed from git, by combinations thereof)

[coap test of ariel os]: https://github.com/ariel-os/ariel-os/tree/main/tests/coap
[coap-scroll-ring-server rust crate]: https://crates.io/crates/coap-scroll-ring-server
[riot os]: https://riot-os.org/
[stdio example]: https://gitlab.com/etonomy/riot-module-examples/-/blob/master/riot-coap-handler-demos/src/stdio.rs?ref_type=heads

functions.name: coap_console.read_stream_to_console
documentation:
Read data from a stream URI that is a variant of
<https://forum.riot-os.org/t/coap-remote-shell/3340/5>. This is (for lack
of implementations) not yet expecting to use observation. Consequently, it
will never see recoverable gaps.

(If the polling fails due to overflows, it's already too late; if the
polling fails due to simultaneous writing, it'll get an error, and recover
from the start of the available data. Only an observation with lost
messages, or one where the server decides to rather send out the latest
data, would result in recoverable gaps.)

The polling will usually apply poll in intervals, exponentially backing off
from the RTT to at most the given `max_poll` value in seconds. but if data
arrives at the waker, eg. because the user has triggered something, the
waker can be used to speed things up.

functions.name: coap_console.autodiscover
documentation:
Given a host URI, read /.well-known/core to discover stdout (eg. by its
if="tag:riot-os.org,2021:ser-out") and stdin
(if="tag:riot-os.org,2021:ser-out"), or more precise attributes (which are
currently not implemented)

classes.name: coap_console.AutoDiscoveryError
documentation: Error with a user friendly message raised when autodiscovery does not succeed

classes.name: coap_console.UriWithRemote

methods.name: coap_console.UriWithRemote.set_on_request
documentation: Modify message to use self as URI, asserting the correctness of the remote

methods.name: coap_console.UriWithRemote.learn_from_response
documentation: Modify self to store the remote from a response message

URI with an aiocoap remote attached

The remote needs to encode the URI's Origin when queried back.
